data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw net outflows of $360 million this week, marking the fourth consecutive week of net outflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a net outflow of $360 million this week (February 9th to February 13th, Eastern Time). The BlackRock ETF IBIT experienced the largest net outflow this week, with a weekly net outflow of $235 million. IBIT's historical total net inflow is currently $61.61 billion. This is followed by the Fidelity ETF FBTC, with a weekly net outflow of $125 million. FBTC's historical total net inflow is currently $10.98 billion. The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest net inflow this week was the Grayscale Bitcoin Mini Trust BTC, with a weekly net inflow of $110 million. BTC's historical total net inflow is currently $2.05 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $87.04 billion, the ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Bitcoin's total market capitalization) is 6.33%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached $54.33 billion.

data.title: The SOL spot ETF saw a net inflow of $13.1706 million this week.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, the SOL spot ETF saw a net inflow of $13.1706 million this week (February 9th to February 13th, Eastern Time). The SOL spot ETF with the largest net inflow this week was the Bitwise ETF BSOL, with a weekly net inflow of $12.7196 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $683 million. This was followed by the Fidelity ETF FSOL, with a weekly net inflow of $732,000, bringing its historical total net inflow to $159 million. The SOL spot ETF with the largest net outflow this week was the VanEck ETF VSOL, with a weekly net outflow of $554,100, bringing its historical total net inflow to $20.21 million. As of press time, the total net asset value of the SOL spot ETF is $721 million, the ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of total SOL market capitalization) is 1.50%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached $875 million.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw net outflows of $161 million this week, marking the fourth consecutive week of net outflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a net outflow of $161 million this week (February 9th to February 13th, Eastern Time). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest net outflow this week was BlackRock ETF ETHA, with a weekly net outflow of $113 million. ETHA's historical total net inflow is currently $11.98 billion. This was followed by Fidelity ETF FETH, with a weekly net outflow of $40.75 million. FETH's historical total net inflow is currently $2.47 billion. The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest net inflow this week was Grayscale Ethereum Mini Trust ETH, with a weekly net inflow of $49.9 million. ETH's historical total net inflow is currently $1.71 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of the Ethereum spot ETF is $11.72 billion, the ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Ethereum's total market capitalization) is 4.75%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached $11.65 billion.

data.title: XRP spot ETF saw a net inflow of $7.6508 million this week.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, XRP spot ETFs saw a net inflow of $7.65 million this week (February 9th to February 13th, Eastern Time). The XRP spot ETF with the largest net inflow this week was the Franklin ETF XRPZ, with a weekly net inflow of $5.4175 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $328 million. This was followed by the Canary ETF XRPC, with a weekly net inflow of $4.2091 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $413 million. The XRP spot ETF with the largest net outflow this week was the Grayscale XRP Trust GXRP, with a weekly net outflow of $5.897 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $131 million. As of press time, the total net asset value of the XRP spot ETF is US$1.01 billion, the ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of XRP's total market capitalization) is 1.18%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached US$1.23 billion.

data.title: Aave faces SEC review as Grayscale files 2.5% fee ETF
data.description:
Grayscale AAVE ETF filing outlines a 2. 5% sponsor fee; analysts cite SEC scrutiny of governance tokens, custody/redemption mechanics, and liquidity as key risks.
source: https://coincu.com/news/aave-faces-sec-review-as-grayscale-files-2-5-fee-etf/

data.title: XRP Spot ETFs Add 3.19M XRP in February 13 Inflows
data.description:
The February 13 flow breakdown shows capital moving selectively rather than broadly across all XRP spot ETFs. Total daily net inflow reached 3. 19 million XRP, with activity concentrated in three issuers while two funds recorded no new positioning. The distribution of flows, both in XRP terms and dollar value, provides a clearer picture of where […] The post XRP Spot ETFs Add 3
source: https://www.ethnews.com/xrp-spot-etfs-add-3-19m-xrp-in-february-13-inflows/

data.title: $736M Short Liquidation Marks Largest Wipeout Since 2024
data.description:
A recent derivatives event revealed positioning pressure that had been quietly building beneath the surface. According to data shared by CryptoQuant, approximately $736 million in short positions were liquidated in a single session, the largest short wipeout since September 20, 2024, when daily liquidations reached $773 million.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/736m-short-liquidation-marks-largest-wipeout-since-2024/

data.title: Morgan Stanley Seeks Blockchain Engineer to Enhance Interoperability
data.description: Morgan Stanley is actively recruiting a blockchain software engineer to spearhead projects aimed at integrating various blockchain technologies, including Hyperledger, Polygon, Canton, and Ethereum. According to NS3.AI, the position offers a salary of up to $150,000 annually, reflecting the growing institutional interest in blockchain interoperability and enterprise-grade solutions. This initiative underscores the financial institution's commitment to advancing blockchain capabilities and fostering innovation in the sector.

data.title: XT will launch ESPUSDT U-margined perpetual contracts.
data.description: XT.COM will launch ESPUSDT U-margined perpetual contracts on February 15, 2026 at 16:00 (UTC+8), supporting leverage up to 50x. It is understood that ESPUSDT contracts do not currently support order placement. XT stated that it will continue to launch more contract trading pairs and related promotions in the future.

data.title: Elon Musk’s X to Launch Smart Cashtags Enabling In-App Stock and Crypto Trading
data.description:
Elon Musk’s social media platform X is preparing to roll out a feature that could transform the app from a discussion forum into a trading venue.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/elon-musks-x-to-launch-smart-cashtags-enabling-in-app-stock-and-crypto-trading/

data.title: X launches Smart Cashtags for crypto, stock trading
data.description:
X will launch Smart Cashtags feature in mid-to-late February 2026 enabling crypto and stock trading. The platform will provide trading links and data tools but will not act as broker or execute trades.
source: https://coinpaprika.com/news/x-launches-smart-cashtags-crypto-stock-trading

data.title: BNB Surpasses 640 USDT with a 3.31% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Feb 15, 2026, 07:37 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has crossed the 640 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 640.76001 USDT, with a narrowed 3.31% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: Grok 4.2 slips after Musk’s ‘next week’ claim
data.description:
We verify Musk's 'next week' claim and map the credible Grok 4. 2 release date window; reasons include safety testing, infrastructure and alignment delays.

data.title: Backpack CEO: In a bear market, retail investors are less likely to get fleeced by TGE (Trading on the Investor) strategies.
data.description: Backpack CEO Armani Ferrante stated in an article on X that TGE is less likely to result in retail investors being exploited compared to TGE in a bull market.

data.title: The "whale" that has accumulated long positions of 105,000 ETH has partially taken profits and currently still holds long positions of 77,000 ETH.
data.description: On-chain analyst Ai Yi monitored that some long positions totaling 105,000 ETH have taken profits. In the past 24 hours, one address closed its long positions of 28,000 ETH, realizing a profit of $1.263 million, with an average closing price of $2094.02. This address currently holds 32,000 ETH, with a floating profit of $1.193 million. Furthermore, this whale also placed limit sell orders of 3000 ETH in the 2100-2110 range. The two addresses currently hold a total of 77,000 ETH in long positions, with a total value of $160 million.

data.title: A whale address that went long on ETH at 20x leverage has turned a profit after previously incurring a paper loss of $3.5 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, the whale address “0x6C8” started going long on ETH on February 11, and once lost more than $3.5 million. It is now making a profit of $24,800 and still holds a long position in ETH (20x leverage), worth $93.79 million.
